questions.text: The sum of all interior angles of a quadrilateral is:
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral can be divided into 2 triangles. Sum = 2 x 180 = 360 degrees.

questions.text: A parallelogram is a quadrilateral with:
questions.explanation: A parallelogram has both pairs of opposite sides parallel and equal.

questions.text: The diagonals of a parallelogram:

options.d: bisect each other at right angles

questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, the diagonals bisect each other (cut each other at their midpoints).

questions.text: A rectangle is a parallelogram with:
questions.explanation: A rectangle is a special parallelogram where every angle is 90 degrees.

questions.text: A rhombus is a parallelogram with:
questions.explanation: A rhombus is a parallelogram in which all four sides are equal.

questions.text: A square is a quadrilateral with:

options.a: all sides equal and all angles 90 degrees

questions.explanation: A square has all four sides equal AND all four angles equal to 90 degrees.

questions.text: In a parallelogram, opposite angles are:
questions.explanation: Opposite angles in a parallelogram are always equal.

questions.text: In a parallelogram, consecutive angles (adjacent angles) are:
questions.explanation: Consecutive angles in a parallelogram are supplementary (add up to 180 degrees).

questions.text: The diagonals of a rectangle are:
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a rectangle are always equal in length.

questions.text: The diagonals of a rhombus:

options.b: bisect each other at right angles

questions.explanation: The diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at 90 degrees (perpendicular bisectors of each other).

questions.text: A trapezium has:

options.b: exactly one pair of parallel sides

questions.explanation: A trapezium has exactly one pair of parallel sides (called the parallel sides or bases).

questions.text: The Midpoint Theorem states that the segment joining midpoints of two sides of a triangle is:

options.b: half the third side and parallel to it

questions.explanation: Midpoint Theorem: the segment joining midpoints of two sides of a triangle is parallel to the third side and half its length.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, angle A = 70 degrees. Find angle C:
questions.explanation: Opposite angles in a parallelogram are equal: angle C = angle A = 70 degrees.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, angle A = 70 degrees. Find angle B:
questions.explanation: Consecutive angles are supplementary: angle B = 180 - 70 = 110 degrees.

questions.text: The diagonals of a square:

options.b: are equal and bisect each other at right angles

questions.explanation: A square combines properties of rectangle (equal diagonals) and rhombus (perpendicular diagonals).

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, diagonal AC = 10 cm and diagonals bisect each other at O. Find AO:
questions.explanation: Diagonals bisect each other: AO = AC / 2 = 5 cm.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: A trapezium has only one pair of parallel sides, so it is not a parallelogram.

questions.text: A quadrilateral with all sides equal but angles not necessarily 90 degrees is:
questions.explanation: A rhombus has all sides equal but its angles need not be 90 degrees.

questions.text: The number of diagonals in a quadrilateral is:
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral has exactly 2 diagonals connecting opposite vertices.

questions.text: In a parallelogram, if one angle is a right angle, then all angles are right angles. Such a parallelogram is called:
questions.explanation: If one angle of a parallelogram is 90 degrees, consecutive angles are also 90 degrees (supplementary), making it a rectangle.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, angle A = 3x degrees and angle B = (2x + 30) degrees. Find x:
questions.explanation: Consecutive angles are supplementary: 3x + 2x + 30 = 180, 5x = 150, x = 30.

questions.text: In a rhombus, the diagonals are 10 cm and 24 cm. Find the side of the rhombus:
questions.explanation: Half diagonals = 5 and 12. Side = sqrt(5^2 + 12^2) = sqrt(25 + 144) = sqrt(169) = 13 cm.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, AB = 8 cm and AD = 5 cm. Find the perimeter:
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2(AB + AD) = 2(8 + 5) = 2 x 13 = 26 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are midpoints of AB and AC. If DE = 4 cm, find BC:
questions.explanation: By Midpoint Theorem: DE = BC / 2, so BC = 2 x DE = 8 cm.

questions.text: Three angles of a quadrilateral are 60, 80 and 120 degrees. Find the fourth angle:
questions.explanation: Sum of angles = 360 degrees. Fourth angle = 360 - 60 - 80 - 120 = 100 degrees.

questions.text: In rectangle ABCD, angle ABD = 35 degrees. Find angle ADB:
questions.explanation: In triangle ABD, angle A = 90 degrees. So angle ABD + angle ADB = 90 degrees. angle ADB = 90 - 35 = 55 degrees.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, angle A = 60 degrees. Find angle D:
questions.explanation: angle A and angle D are consecutive angles: angle D = 180 - 60 = 120 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, M and N are midpoints of PQ and QR. If MN = 6 cm, find PR:
questions.explanation: By Midpoint Theorem: MN = PR / 2, so PR = 2 x MN = 12 cm.

questions.text: In rhombus ABCD, angle A = 60 degrees. Find angle B:
questions.explanation: Consecutive angles in a rhombus are supplementary: angle B = 180 - 60 = 120 degrees.

questions.text: In parallelogram PQRS, diagonals bisect each other at O. If PO = 5 cm and QO = 7 cm, find PR and QS:
questions.explanation: Diagonals bisect each other: PR = 2 x PO = 10 cm and QS = 2 x QO = 14 cm.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, angle A = (2x + 15) degrees and angle C = (3x - 20) degrees. Find angle A:
questions.explanation: Opposite angles are equal: 2x + 15 = 3x - 20, x = 35. angle A = 2(35) + 15 = 85 degrees.

questions.text: The diagonals of a rhombus are in ratio 3:4 and its perimeter is 40 cm. Find the longer diagonal:
questions.explanation: Side = 40/4 = 10 cm. Half diagonals are 3k and 4k. (3k)^2 + (4k)^2 = 100, 25k^2 = 100, k = 2. Longer diagonal = 2 x 4k = 16 cm.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, X and Y are midpoints of AB and CD respectively. AXCY is a:
questions.explanation: AX = AB/2 = CD/2 = CY and AX || CY (since AB || CD). So AXCY has one pair of equal parallel sides and is a parallelogram.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D, E, F are midpoints of AB, BC and CA. The ratio of area of triangle DEF to triangle ABC is:
questions.explanation: By Midpoint Theorem, triangle DEF is similar to triangle ABC with scale factor 1/2. Area ratio = (1/2)^2 = 1:4.

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, AB = CD and AB || CD. What type of quadrilateral is it?
questions.explanation: If one pair of opposite sides is both equal and parallel, the quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, angle A = 2 times angle B. Find angle A:
questions.explanation: Consecutive angles supplementary: angle A + angle B = 180. 2 angle B + angle B = 180, angle B = 60. angle A = 2 x 60 = 120 degrees.

questions.text: Three angles of a quadrilateral are 80, 110 and 95 degrees. Find the fourth angle (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Fourth angle = 360 - 80 - 110 - 95 = 75 degrees.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, angle A = 65 degrees. Find angle B (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Consecutive angles supplementary: angle B = 180 - 65 = 115 degrees.

questions.text: In a rhombus, the diagonals are 6 cm and 8 cm. Find the length of each side (in cm).
questions.explanation: Half diagonals = 3 and 4. Side = sqrt(3^2 + 4^2) = sqrt(9 + 16) = sqrt(25) = 5 cm.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, AB = 12 cm and BC = 7 cm. Find the perimeter (in cm).
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2(AB + BC) = 2(12 + 7) = 38 cm.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, D and E are midpoints of PQ and PR. If DE = 5 cm, find QR (in cm).
questions.explanation: By Midpoint Theorem: DE = QR / 2, so QR = 2 x 5 = 10 cm.

questions.text: One diagonal of a parallelogram is 14 cm. The diagonals bisect each other at O. Find OB where B is one endpoint of this diagonal (in cm).
questions.explanation: Diagonals bisect each other: OB = 14 / 2 = 7 cm.

questions.text: In rectangle ABCD, angle ABD = 40 degrees. Find angle BDC (in degrees).
questions.explanation: In triangle ABD, angle A = 90, angle ABD = 40, so angle ADB = 50. angle ADC = 90, so angle BDC = 90 - 50 = 40 degrees.

questions.text: In rhombus ABCD, angle A = 70 degrees. Find angle B (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Consecutive angles supplementary: angle B = 180 - 70 = 110 degrees.

questions.text: In parallelogram ABCD, angle A = 2 times angle B. Find angle A (in degrees).
questions.explanation: angle A + angle B = 180. 2 angle B + angle B = 180, angle B = 60. angle A = 120 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, D and E are midpoints of AB and AC. If BC = 18 cm, find DE (in cm).
questions.explanation: By Midpoint Theorem: DE = BC / 2 = 18 / 2 = 9 cm.

cases.case_text: A farmer has a rectangular field ABCD with length AB = 60 m and width BC = 40 m. He wants to find the length of the diagonal fencing across the field and verify some geometric properties. He also marks the midpoints P and Q of sides AB and CD respectively to divide the field into smaller sections for different crops.

subparts.text: In rectangle ABCD, if angle ABD = 30 degrees, find angle ADB (in degrees):
subparts.explanation: In triangle ABD, angle A = 90, so angle ABD + angle ADB = 90. angle ADB = 90 - 30 = 60 degrees.

subparts.text: The diagonals of rectangle ABCD bisect each other at O. If AC = 50 m, find OA (in metres):
subparts.explanation: Diagonals bisect each other: OA = AC / 2 = 50 / 2 = 25 m.

subparts.text: Find the perimeter of the rectangular field (in metres).

cases.case_text: A garden is designed in the shape of a rhombus ABCD where all sides are 10 m. The gardener is digging paths along the two diagonals AC and BD. The diagonals intersect at point O. The longer diagonal AC = 16 m. The gardener needs to find the length of the shorter diagonal and verify angle properties.

subparts.text: Find the length of the shorter diagonal BD (in metres) using the Pythagorean theorem. (Side = 10 m, AO = 8 m):
The diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles. The angle at O between AC and BD is:
subparts.explanation: The diagonals of a rhombus are perpendicular bisectors of each other: angle at O = 90 degrees.

subparts.text: Find the perimeter of the rhombus garden (in metres).
subparts.explanation: Perimeter of rhombus = 4 x side = 4 x 10 = 40 m.
